title: fermented silky indian wild garlic leaves korma
description: a rich and creamy korma sauce with fermented wild garlic leaves, served silky and smooth over basmati rice. this dish is a showcase of indian cuisine's subtlety and depth, while being nut-free and easy to make.

ingredients.name: wild garlic leaves
ingredients.notes: lightly packed, stems removed, and chopped into 1-inch pieces

ingredients.name: coconut milk
ingredients.notes: full-fat, chilled in the fridge overnight

ingredients.name: fermented rice paste
ingredients.notes: homemade or store-bought, see notes for recipe

instructions: in a blender or food processor, combine the fermented rice paste, coconut milk, ghee or oil, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, and turmeric. blend until smooth and creamy.
transfer the mixture to a saucepan and bring to a simmer over medium heat.
reduce the heat to low and let it cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
while the sauce is cooking, heat a large skillet over medium heat and add the chopped wild garlic leaves. cook until wilted, about 3-4 minutes.
add the cooked wild garlic to the saucepan and stir to combine.
serve the korma sauce over basmati rice, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro.

note: for homemade fermented rice paste, combine 1 cup of cooked rice with 1 cup of water in a bowl. cover and let it sit at room temperature for 24-48 hours, or until it develops a sour smell and a slightly effervescent texture.
